textPlain: > In her new book, Beth Macy returns to her home town of Urbana, Ohio, using it as a ground zero for understanding right-wing radicalization.https://archive.ph/eUs98

Urbana isn't even that dead for a small town. It's a county seat. 11,000 people. Still has a high school. Had a university until 2000. There's some industry left.The big milestones in the death of a town are when the high school closes, and, finally, when the post office closes.
